my_thoughts: The shocking metaphor works: moon is hungry (anthropomorphic metaphor). The student asks esoteric question (seeking esoteric explanation), which shows they understand: soul = goes to moon (mechanism). They'll likely test boundary conditions next. Hypothesis: shocking metaphors trigger boundary tests—testable by tracking whether shocking metaphors lead to boundary inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student asks esoteric question ('Why does the soul at death go to the moon?')—seeking esoteric explanation via asks theological question and seeks mechanism. They understand: soul = goes to moon (mechanism). The structural_analogy mechanism gives: shocking answer (moon is hungry), uses: anthropomorphic metaphor (moon = hungry). Pattern: esoteric question → shocking metaphor → [next: boundary test]. Low confidence because shocking metaphors can lead to deeper questions or boundary tests.
